_provenance.stage: Stage 1 — derived from live cards for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: Suaram: Up to 28% of SOSMA Detainees Released Without Charge, From Nearly 7,000 Arrests Since 2018
context: Suaram's 2025 report documented 6,892 SOSMA arrests between 2018 and 2023. Up to 28% were eventually released without being charged. The 28-day no-charge detention runs at scale on people never prosecuted.

cards.text: Suaram: 6,892 SOSMA arrests in 5 years, up to 28% released without ever being charged.
cards.sub: Of arrests under the Security Offences (Special Measures) Act 2012 between 2018 and 2023, roughly one in four did not result in any charge being filed at all.

cards.h: SOSMA Section 4 allows 28 days of detention without charge since 2012, replacing the ISA but retaining preventive-detention powers.
cards.s: The 28-day window can be used while authorities decide whether to charge. The 28% release-without-charge figure shows how often the answer is 'no'.
cards.d: SOSMA Section 4 allows 28 days of detention without charge since 2012, replacing the ISA but retaining preventive-detention powers. The 28-day window can be used while authorities decide whether to charge. The 28% release-without-charge figure shows how often the answer is 'no'.

cards.h: SUHAKAM has run public inquiries into custodial conditions, most recently a 28-day inquiry into torture and death in custody at Taiping Prison.
cards.s: Custodial-death inquiries provide one of the few public windows into how preventive detention functions in practice.
cards.d: SUHAKAM has run public inquiries into custodial conditions, most recently a 28-day inquiry into torture and death in custody at Taiping Prison. Custodial-death inquiries provide one of the few public windows into how preventive detention functions in practice.

cards.h: Suaram's annual reports remain the most consistent civil-society source on SOSMA usage. Official Home Ministry figures are not released in disaggregated form.
cards.s: The asymmetry between civil-society reporting and government disclosure is itself a structural transparency gap.
cards.d: Suaram's annual reports remain the most consistent civil-society source on SOSMA usage. Official Home Ministry figures are not released in disaggregated form. The asymmetry between civil-society reporting and government disclosure is itself a structural transparency gap.

cards.text: SOSMA is no longer a rare emergency tool. It is a routine pre-charge detention regime that releases one in four detainees uncharged.
Like a 28-day holding cell where one in four occupants is released with no charge, and there is no public log of who entered or why.
cards.sub: Pre-charge detention used at scale, with limited public accountability for the cases that never produce charges.

cards.text: Genuine SOSMA reform requires disaggregated public reporting on arrests, durations, charges and outcomes, not just renewal debates every five years.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
